ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general lemon tea food product node; no brand-specific Heaven & Earth entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a brand-specific Heaven & Earth Lemon Tea code. AN0KL (tea-based beverages, flavoured) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226808007 covers lemon tea as a substance category; no brand-specific code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one 500 ml can or bottle of Heaven & Earth Lemon Tea, which is the typical single-serve retail format sold in Singapore convenience stores and food courts.

nutrient_source.title: Heaven & Earth Lemon Tea — Nutrition Information Panel (product label) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central: Lemon-flavored iced tea, ready-to-drink (FDC ID 1104078)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Branded Foods) / Coca-Cola Singapore product label
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Heaven & Earth Lemon Tea is a Coca-Cola Singapore branded RTD lemon tea. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central branded lemon-flavoured iced tea entries and cross-checked against the product's Singapore nutrition information panel (per 100 ml basis). The product is a sweetened, lemon-flavoured black tea beverage with approximately 8–9 g sugar per 100 ml and negligible protein and fat. Values are expressed per 100 g (approximately equivalent to 100 ml for this beverage).

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sugar-sweetened beverages and lemon-flavoured tea drinks — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened iced tea and sucrose-containing beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Heaven & Earth Lemon Tea specifically. The GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened iced tea and sucrose-containing soft drinks, which cluster in the GI range 47–65. A midpoint value of 55 (low GI) is applied. Glycemic load per 500 ml serving = (55 × 42.5 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 23.4, where 42.5 g is the carbohydrate content of a 500 ml serving (8.5 g per 100 ml × 5).

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~10 mg per 100 ml); primary concern is added sugar rather than sodium
hypertension.guidance: Heaven & Earth Lemon Tea is low in sodium and does not directly elevate blood pressure through salt load, but regular consumption of sweetened beverages contributes to weight gain and metabolic risk that can worsen hypertension over time; limit to occasional intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content (~8 g per 100 ml; ~40 g per 500 ml serving) with low-medium glycemic index (~55) producing a high glycemic load per serving (~23)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Heaven & Earth Lemon Tea due to its high free sugar content and significant glycemic load per serving, which can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; a 500 ml serving delivers approximately 40 g of rapidly absorbed sugars.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and sodium, but high added sugar intake is associated with elevated triglycerides and increased cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While Heaven & Earth Lemon Tea contains no fat or cholesterol, its high added sugar content (~40 g per 500 ml serving) is associated with elevated serum triglycerides and increased cardiovascular disease risk when consumed regularly; individuals with CVD should treat this as an occasional beverage only.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Heaven & Earth Lemon Tea is a popular ready-to-drink (RTD) sweetened lemon-flavoured black tea beverage produced by Coca-Cola Singapore under the Heaven & Earth brand. It is widely sold across Singapore in 500 ml cans and bottles through convenience stores, supermarkets, hawker centres, and food courts, and is commonly served as an accompaniment to meals. The beverage is a blend of black tea extract, water, sugar, lemon juice or lemon flavouring, and citric acid, giving it a light amber colour, a mildly astringent tea base, and a sweet-tart citrus finish. It is consumed chilled and is not typically heated. Nutritionally, it is a high-sugar, low-nutrient beverage providing approximately 34 kcal per 100 ml, with around 8–8.5 g of sugar per 100 ml and negligible protein, fat, and dietary fibre. A standard 500 ml serving delivers approximately 170 kcal and 40–42 g of free sugars, which exceeds the World Health Organization's recommended daily free sugar limit of 25 g for adults. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low GI) based on analogous sweetened tea and sucrose-containing beverage data from the University of Sydney GI Database, yielding a high glycemic load per serving of approximately 23. Clinically, this beverage is of high concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its rapid glycemic impact, of moderate concern for cardiovascular disease due to its contribution to elevated triglycerides, and of low direct concern for hypertension due to its minimal sodium content. It is vegetarian, vegan, halal-suitable, gluten-free, and dairy-free. The product is classified as ultra-processed under the NOVA food classification system.

confidence_reason: Heaven & Earth Lemon Tea is a branded commercial product sold in Singapore. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central branded lemon-flavoured iced tea entries and cross-referenced with the product's Singapore nutrition information panel. No exact HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for this specific branded product. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. protein_g is 0.0: confirmed zero — the product's Singapore nutrition information panel explicitly reports 0 g protein per serving, consistent with USDA branded lemon iced tea data. This is naturally zero for a tea-based sugar-water beverage with no protein-containing ingredients. 2. fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero — naturally zero by product formulation; no fat-containing ingredients are present. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all 0.0 confirmed zero by formulation. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero for a filtered liquid beverage with no fibre-containing solids. 4. iron_mg is -1: not reported in the USDA FoodData Central branded lemon iced tea source or on the Singapore product label. 5. vitamin_c_mg is -1: not reported in the primary source; lemon flavouring may contribute trace amounts but this is not quantified in the approved source. 6. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are all -1: not reported in the approved source for this product category. 7. glycemic_index is an analogous estimate of 55 (low GI) based on University of Sydney GI Database data for sweetened iced tea and sucrose-containing beverages; no direct GI measurement for this specific branded product was found in the approved GI source. 8. The cuisine field in the input was listed as 'Fries' which is clearly a field survey platform categorisation artefact; this has been corrected to 'Beverages' based on the actual product identity. 9. Serving size of 500 ml is the standard single-serve retail format for this product in Singapore.
